chunks.title: Introduction to Fruit Juice
chunks.summary: The podcast begins with the host discussing the controversial topic of fruit juice, arguing for its inclusion in the diet. He mentions the existence of research supporting the health benefits of fruit juice while addressing concerns about sugar and insulin spikes.
chunks.transcript: but as you'll see in this podcast there's a large amount of research to suggest that fruit juice is healthy for humans on this week's podcast I talk about a very controversial topic it might get me banned from YouTube no it's not actually that controversial but it is fruit juice which sends people in all sorts of different camps into a tizzy and I make an argument for why you should include fruit juice in your diet I will provide many studies in this podcast that show benefits of fruit juice I will talk about whether you should worry about glucose spikes whether you should worry about insulin the benefits of eating glucose the benefits of including carbohydrates in your diet specifically carbohydrates from sugar containing foods like honey and fruit which have been widely vilified but I think have a very indispensable valuable place in the human diet and always have evolutionarily so I hope this podcast is helpful for many of you again as I say in the podcast I'm always open to other ideas I would love to have people on the show who have differing ideas than mine it's just been difficult to get them to engage with many of these conversations so my belief is that fruit fruit juice and honey are beneficial for humans in the setting of an animal-based diet a diet that is composed and centered around animal foods like well-raised meat grass-fed grass-finished meat organs especially fresh or organs from hard and soil supplements desiccated organs these are all valuable parts of an animal-based diet along with fruit honey and raw dairy which is delicious something I've talked about a previous podcast so I hope this podcast is helpful for you and then after this podcast you will include a little more fruit juice in your diet and reap some of the benefits of this valuable type of food I also believe that there's been a lot of discussion around fearing glucose spikes and I wanted to attack this head-on because I think this is not something humans should generally be afraid of so enjoy this podcast guys

chunks.title: The Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The host explains his perspective on an animal-based diet, emphasizing the importance of including quality animal foods, fruits, and honey. He shares personal experiences regarding dietary changes that led to health improvements after incorporating carbohydrates back into his diet.
chunks.transcript: many of you will know that my story began with eczema and asthma when I was eating a paleo diet an organic Paleo Diet with salads and nuts and fruit and meat and mushrooms this kind of stuff olive oil that got better when I cut out all of the plant foods for my diet and did a strictly carnivore diet of meat and organs and animal fat and salt for about a year and a half that resulted in some pretty significant electrolyte variabilities electrolyte deficiencies leading to muscle cramps sleep disturbances including hypnagogic jerks like when I would go to sleep I would have these jerks your body jerks you awake this is related to a magnesium deficiency testosterone declining from what my Baseline is usually seven to eight hundred as a 46 year old man on no hormone replacement to high 400s at times and like I said muscle cramps preventing me from doing things like rock climbing Etc so at that point I added honey and fruit back to my diet and began to feel gradually better I've been eating an animal-based diet which is a term that I've used to characterize a diet that I think is more evolutionarily consistent for humans than simply eating just meat and organs though I think meat and organs are the center of the human diet all hunter-gatherer tribes will consume carbohydrates when they're presented with them East hunter-gatherer tribes are equatorial so these carbohydrates are present year round certainly spending time with hadza reminded me of this they ate lots of honey when we could get our hands on it fruit and we could get our hands on it unquestionably they were doing this adding fruit and honey back to my diet significantly improved all those issues I've done multiple podcasts looking at my blood work showing that my testosterone is quite high for a 46 year old again seven eight hundred
